caseStudy.title: Enterprise Knowledge Management
caseStudy.description: AI-powered internal knowledge retrieval for a 20,000-person org.
caseStudy.overview: A global enterprise struggled with institutional knowledge fragmented across wikis, SharePoint sites, and email threads. Employees spent significant time searching for internal documentation, reducing productivity and slowing onboarding for new hires.
caseStudy.problem: No single source of truth existed for internal processes, policies, or technical documentation. Keyword search returned thousands of irrelevant results, and critical institutional knowledge lived exclusively in the heads of long-tenured employees.
caseStudy.solution: A semantic search system was built on top of the existing document repositories, using vector embeddings to understand intent rather than keywords. An internal assistant interface allowed employees to ask natural-language questions and receive direct answers with source citations. Content was automatically re-indexed on a nightly schedule.

results.summary: Knowledge retrieval is now fast and accurate, enabling employees to self-serve on most queries without escalating to subject matter experts. Onboarding programs were redesigned around the new tool.
